query: What is the species of cockroach found in Spain?

topRanked.articleTitle: A review of the ensign wasps (Hymenoptera: Evaniidae) from Macaronesia

images.captionText: FIGURE 1. Brightfield photographs of a female Evania appendigaster (Linneaus, 1758). a) Dorsal view of head. b) Anterior view of the head. c) Lateral view of the head. d) Lateral view of the mesosoma. e) Dorsal view of the mesosoma. f) Dorsal view of the right wings. g) Lateral view of the abdomen.

FIGURE 3. Distribution records of Evania appendigaster (Linnaeus, 1758) across the Macaronesian archipelagos, based on specimens examined and data from citizen science platforms: a) Madeira archipelago. b) Canary Islands. c) Cape Verde archipelago. The colours represent the number of records on each island.

images.captionText: Figure 12. Obama nungara sp. nov. Un-numbered specimen from Guernsey creeping in dorsal view. The ground marks are 1 cm from each other.
Figure 13. Obama nungara sp. nov. Un-numbered specimen from Guernsey showing part of the ventral side. The ground marks are 1 cm from each other.
Figure 14. Obama nungara sp. nov. Dorsal view of an un-numbered specimen from Guernsey. The ground marks are 1 cm from each other.
Figure 15. Obama nungara sp. nov. A region anterior to the pharynx of specimen MZUSP PL 1599 in clove oil.
Figure 16. Obama nungara sp. nov. Anterior region of paratype MZUSP PL 1595 in clove oil.
Figure 17. Obama nungara sp. nov. Diagrammatic representation of the pharynx of paratype MZUSP PL 1066 in lateral view.

Figure 18 – 23. Obama nungara sp. nov. Series of sagittal sections of the copulatory apparatus of the holotype from the left (Fig. 17) to the right side (Fig. 22) of the body. Same magnification for all.

Figure 25. Obama nungara sp. nov. Photomicrograph of a detail of penis of specimen F 6108. Black arrowheads point to bundles of penis glands discharging pinkishstained secretion into the male atrium. White arrowheads point to penis glands producing reddish-stained secretion.
Figure 26. Obama nungara sp. nov. Photomicrograph of a detail of penis of specimen MZUSP PL 1066. Arrowhead points to a bundle of glands discharging pinkish-stained secretion into the male atrium.
Figure 27. Obama nungara sp. nov. Detail of penis of specimen MZUSP PL 1595. Arrowhead points to a bundle of glands discharging pinkish secretion into the male atrium.

Figure 24. Obama nungara sp. nov. Photomicrograph of a sagittal section of the copulatory apparatus of specimen F 6108. Black arrowheads point to bundles of penis glands containg pinkish-stained secretion. White arrowheads point to penis glands producing reddish-stained secretion.
Figure 28. Obama nungara sp. nov. Photomicrograph of a sagittal section of the copulatory apparatus of specimen MZUSP PL 1066.
Figure 29. Obama nungara sp. nov. Diagrammatic representation of the copulatory apparatus of specimen MZUSP PL 1596 from sagittal sections.

response.answer: The species of cockroach identified in Spain is "Obama nungara." This species was found from a specimen collected in Málaga, a city in Spain. The discussion also mentions that specimen F6108 was sent to Barcelona, another Spanish-speaking country, suggesting further information about its origin there. Therefore, the primary species of cockroach found in Spain includes "Obama nungara."
